title: cornutia grandifolia var. normalis
intro: Cornutia grandifolia var. normalis, also known as the Cornuta or Large-leaved Cornuta, is a plant species belonging to the family Gelsemiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cornutia grandifolia var. normalis include reducing fever, treating wounds and skin infections, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Cornutia grandifolia var. normalis is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal purposes, and its essential oils are also used in traditional medicine and aromatherapy.
